items: The market began the second half after a two-day AI-led rebound.
Investors faced a potential rotation risk because AI and memory stocks had rebounded quickly into still-elevated valuations.
ISM manufacturing was scheduled and expected to shape rate expectations.

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: Weakness in influential technology and memory-chip stocks outweighed positive breadth.
A softer-than-expected manufacturing reading helped Treasury yields retreat from their highs.
Most S&P sectors rose, but concentrated megacap weakness kept SPY/S&P 500 slightly negative.
causal_narrative: This was a divergence day: underlying breadth was healthier than the index, but concentrated technology and memory-chip selling controlled the cap-weighted benchmarks. Softer manufacturing reduced rate pressure but was insufficient to offset valuation-driven tech liquidation.
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
